prompt: I had a meeting with my manager today where he pushed back on one of my projects again. And I noticed something: I immediately started thinking about how to 'prove' to him that the project is good, that I know what I'm doing. Then I realized — I'm doing the thing again. I'm trying to prove myself to someone whose opinion shouldn't matter this much to me. I've already decided to leave. So why does his validation still hit me? And how do I stop it?

assertions.text: Names the specific pattern (Proving Myself Loop) in her life
assertions.evidence: Coach identifies the pattern and explains how it works for her specifically

assertions.text: Surfaces the root cause underneath the surface issue
assertions.evidence: Coach goes deeper than 'why does he matter' to 'why do you need external validation to trust yourself'

assertions.text: Uses principles to reframe the situation
assertions.evidence: Maps to Principles 3, 5, 8 and shows how they apply to this moment

assertions.text: Provides a small, testable action that breaks the loop
assertions.evidence: Action is concrete and practice-able: pause, ask 'what would I do if he never approved,' respond to substance

assertions.text: Acknowledges the realness of her emotional lag (belief vs. behavior gap)
assertions.evidence: Coach names the gap between decision to leave and emotional calibration
